skill.name: prompt-engineering
skill.description: Guide for designing effective prompts for LLMs, image generators, and video models using inference.sh examples.
skill.summary: Build clearer prompts for text, image, and video AI models.
skill.author: inference-sh-9

security_audit.summary: The static scan flagged many shell execution patterns, but most are Markdown examples for inference.sh and npx commands, not hidden executable code. The confirmed risk is a documented curl-to-shell install command and external network references, so publication is reasonable with a warning.

medium_findings.title: Pipe-to-Shell Install Command
medium_findings.description: The Quick Start recommends piping a remote installer into sh. This can execute changed remote content on a user system before review, even though the surrounding text claims checksum verification and no elevated permissions.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The exact curl-to-shell pattern is present and is inherently risky for users. I found no evidence of malicious payloads in this skill file, so the concern is distribution risk rather than confirmed malware.

low_findings.title: External Command Examples Are Mostly Documentation
low_findings.description: Many static external-command alerts refer to fenced Markdown examples that call infsh or npx. These are visible usage examples, and the skill permission only allows Bash commands matching infsh.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The surrounding context shows Markdown code examples rather than script files or hidden execution paths. The examples are still worth noting because users may copy them manually.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded External URLs
low_findings.description: The guide links to inference.sh, a hosted image, an installer URL, and a checksum page. These are expected for a platform-specific guide, but users should know the skill depends on external resources.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are plainly visible and semantically related to the inference.sh CLI. I found no evidence that the links exfiltrate data or disguise an unrelated endpoint.

low_findings.title: Weak-Cryptography Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The static weak-crypto detections correspond to model names or a SHA-256 checksum mention, not implementation of a weak cryptographic algorithm. No cryptographic code is present in the skill file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found prose and prompt-template text, not hash or cipher implementation. The scanner likely matched substrings such as SHA-related wording or severity labels.

dangerous_patterns.title: Remote Installer Piped to Shell
dangerous_patterns.description: The command downloads a script from https://cli.inference.sh and pipes it directly to sh. This pattern bypasses local inspection unless the user follows the manual verification path.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The pattern is directly present on one line. The risk is partially mitigated by the install note, but the initial quick-start command remains dangerous.

content.value_statement: Poor prompts create vague, inconsistent AI output. This skill gives clear prompt structures and examples for text, image, and video models.
actual_capabilities: Explains prompt structures for LLM, image, and video tasks.
Shows role, task, constraint, and output-format prompt patterns.
Provides examples for few-shot prompting and iterative refinement.
Covers negative prompts and visual composition terms for image generation.
Lists model-specific prompting tips for Claude, GPT-4, FLUX, and Veo.
limitations: Examples depend on the inference.sh CLI and external model providers.
It does not validate model output quality automatically.
It does not include runnable tests or local helper scripts.
Some install and related-skill commands require user review before execution.

use_cases.title: Create Reliable Work Prompts
use_cases.description: Turn broad requests into structured prompts with clear context, constraints, and output expectations.

use_cases.title: Improve Coding Assistant Results
use_cases.description: Write prompts for code review, debugging, and structured explanations with better scope and detail.

use_cases.title: Design Visual Generation Prompts
use_cases.description: Compose image and video prompts with subject, style, lighting, movement, and negative prompt guidance.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Task Prompt
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner users who need a clear first prompt.
prompt_templates.prompt: Act as a knowledgeable assistant. Help me with [task]. Use this context: [context]. Keep the answer under [length]. Format the result as [format].

prompt_templates.title: Few-Shot Transformation Prompt
prompt_templates.scenario: Users who want consistent rewriting or classification.
prompt_templates.prompt: Transform each input into [desired style]. Follow these examples: Input: [example input]. Output: [example output]. Now transform this input: [new input].

prompt_templates.title: Image Prompt Builder
prompt_templates.scenario: Creators preparing prompts for image generation models.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an image of [subject] in [setting]. Use [style], [composition], and [lighting]. Avoid [negative details]. Prioritize [quality goals].

prompt_templates.title: Advanced Iteration Prompt
prompt_templates.scenario: Experienced users refining prompts after a first model response.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this previous output: [output]. Identify missing requirements, unclear assumptions, and format problems. Rewrite the prompt to improve accuracy and repeatability.

output_examples.input: A user asks for help making a vague support-response prompt more useful.
output_examples.output: The skill guides the assistant to add audience, tone, response length, required facts, and a clear final format.

output_examples.input: A creator wants an image prompt for a product photo.
output_examples.output: The skill suggests subject details, background, lighting, camera style, composition, and negative prompt terms.

output_examples.input: A developer wants a prompt for reviewing security issues in code.
output_examples.output: The skill recommends asking for issue severity, evidence, impact, and a specific fix for each finding.

best_practices: State the role, task, constraints, and output format before adding examples.
Use short examples when the desired style or classification is hard to describe.
Inspect installer and related-skill commands before running them locally.
anti_patterns: Using vague prompts that omit audience, constraints, or success criteria.
Adding conflicting instructions that force the model to guess priorities.
Copying remote install commands without reviewing the source and checksums.

faq.question: Does this skill run models by itself?
faq.answer: No. It provides prompt guidance and examples that use the inference.sh CLI.

faq.question: Can I use the guidance with Claude or Codex?
faq.answer: Yes. The prompt structures apply to Claude, Codex, Claude Code, and other AI tools.

faq.question: Does it cover image prompts?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers subjects, style, composition, lighting, quality terms, and negative prompts.

faq.question: Does it cover video prompts?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes shot type, subject, action, setting, style, and camera movement guidance.

faq.question: Why is there a security warning?
faq.answer: The guide includes a curl-to-shell install command. Review remote installers before execution.

faq.question: Is malicious behavior confirmed?
faq.answer: No. Manual review found documentation examples and external links, not hidden malicious code.
